subject.title: English A Lang & Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Language & Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **the study of language and literature through a wide variety of texts and media from different periods, styles, and cultures**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis of both literary and non-literary texts, examining how language choices create meaning and effect in different contexts**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL) |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 35% (SL), 25% (HL)  |
| **HL Essay** | Written assignment | N/A | 1200-1500 words | 25% (HL only) |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation | 15 minutes | 15 minutes | 30% (SL), 20% (HL) |

strengths: The research question is explicitly stated, sharply focused and consistently revisited throughout the essay.
The topic is communicated with clear purpose and direction, with thematic chapters directly aligned to the research question.
A suitably complete methodology is in evidence, with a coherent combination of close textual analysis and a range of secondary sources.
weaknesses: The opening introduction is occasionally verbose and drifts into general definitions before homing in on the literary focus.
The methodology remains largely implicit and would benefit from a more explicit justification of source selection and research procedures.
Some comparative shifts (e.g. from Grenouille to Beth) lack clear structural signposting which slightly dilutes overall coherence.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Despite minor lapses in concision and explicit methodological justification, the essay demonstrates a clear, effective focus, a tightly connected research question and a complete, well-executed method. These qualities place it securely at the top of the 5â6 band.

strengths: Demonstrates excellent, coherent understanding of both primary texts, with accurate recounting of plot and thematic context.
Terminology such as âcharacterization,â âmotif,â âsymbolismâ and interdisciplinary concepts like âdopamineâ and âsuperiority complexâ are used accurately and consistently.
Secondary sources are effectively integrated to support key claims, showing maturity in application.
weaknesses: Historical and theoretical contexts for the authors are only touched on superficially.
Reliance on a few non-peer-reviewed sources (e.g., YouTube, blogs) weakens the academic foundation.
Some psychological and literary concepts are invoked without sufficient scholarly grounding or precise citation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Overall the student displays an excellent grasp of the texts and uses terminology and sources with strong consistency and precision. Minor gaps in theoretical depth and source rigour are outweighed by the generally sophisticated application, meriting a band-top score.

strengths: The essay offers sustained, focused comparative analysis of the protagonistsâ motivations, childhoods and moral trajectories, backed by well-chosen textual quotations.
Interpretative insights (e.g., linking scent and identity, situating Bethâs struggle within 1960s feminism) demonstrate thoughtful critical evaluation.
The line of argument is coherent, with a clear progression from thematic chapters to an evaluative conclusion.
weaknesses: Counter-arguments and alternative readings are largely absent, limiting the balance of the critical evaluation.
Occasional drift into plot summary interrupts analytical momentum.
Use of popular-media sources in analysis undermines scholarly rigour and would benefit from more critical source evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work moves beyond description into a genuine critical dialogue with the texts, making nuanced comparative observations and well-supported conclusions. Although some deeper interrogation of sources and counter-readings is missing, the response consistently meets the descriptors for the 7â9 band.

strengths: Follows the prescribed extended essay structure with title page, contents, thematic chapters and works-cited list.
Layout features (pagination, consistent font and spacing, declared word count) are correctly applied.
Section headings and clear navigation aid reader orientation.
weaknesses: Inconsistent use of tab leaders and ellipses in the table of contents.
Underlining of headers and uneven hanging indents in the bibliography detract from professional polish.
Some long quotations are not formatted according to style conventions, and paragraphing can be uneven.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is generally clear and meets all formal requirements. Minor inconsistencies in headings, contents formatting and reference indentation prevent a perfect score but nonetheless sit firmly in the 3â4 band.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths**: The essay provides a concise methodology: close textual analysis of primary works supported by secondary sources (academic journals, essays, videos). The works-cited list shows a modest variety of scholarly and popular materials, suggesting informed selection. The structure (title page, table of contents, word count, citations) indicates planning and basic adherence to EE conventions.

**Limitations**: Discussion of methodology remains largely implicit; there is little explicit justification of source selection, theoretical framework, or research procedures beyond stating that secondary sources were âutilized.â Several cited sources are popular (YouTube video, Psychology Today blog) rather than strictly academic, pointing to only partial effectiveness in source range and relevance.

Overall, the methodology is mostly complete and generally relevant but lacks the breadth, rigour, and explicit critical reflection characteristic of the top band. A score of 4 within the 3â4 band is appropriate.

**Strengths**: The essay respects EE formatting norms: word count declared (3999), title and candidate information provided, double-spaced text, consistent font, numbered pages, and a clearly formatted works-cited list. Citations roughly follow MLA style.

**Limitations**: Minor formatting lapses occurâellipses in the table of contents, inconsistent indentation in Works Cited, and occasional long quotations without proper formatting. These issues are noticeable but do not impede comprehension.

Overall, layout and formatting are correctly applied in most respects, meeting the descriptors for the 3â4 band. A score of 3 reflects solid but not flawless execution.

**Strengths**: The bibliography includes primary texts and roughly eight secondary sources spanning journal articles, an academic conference paper, and critical essays, demonstrating an adequate breadth. Most sources relate directly to the themes of power, obsession, feminism, and psychology, indicating reasonable relevance. In-text citations are generally present, showing an effort to integrate research.

**Limitations**: The depth of engagement with these sources is partial; quotations are sometimes used illustratively rather than analytically. The inclusion of less scholarly items (e.g., YouTube video, popular magazine blog) dilutes overall academic rigour. Some claims about psychology or historical context are only lightly supported.

Taken together, the research base is **good and mostly relevant** but falls short of the sustained excellence and critical selectivity of the top bands. A score of 5 within the 4â6 band is warranted.

**Strengths**: The introduction states an explicit topicââenvironmental influences as a driving force behind desiring powerââand immediately anchors it in two specified literary works. The student outlines the thematic focus (obsession with power), signals the key lenses (childhood, alienation, morality), and provides a clear sense of purpose and direction. The writing is coherent and the reader can easily understand what the essay will examine.

**Limitations**: While the topic is clearly communicated, the opening pages are somewhat verbose and occasionally digressive (e.g., long generalisations about power and obsession) before homing in on the literary analysis. A crisper articulation of scope (e.g., precise aspects of âenvironmental influencesâ) would have enhanced precision.

Overall, clarity and focus are strong and purposeful, though not exemplary in concision or specificity, warranting a secure placement in the 5â6 band, with a score of 5.

**Strengths**: The discussion identifies parallels and contrasts between the two novels, culminating in a conclusion that reflects on differing outcomes for the protagonists. The student recognises broader social and psychological implications, offering some evaluation of how environment shapes obsession and morality.

**Limitations**: Evaluation remains basic; the essay rarely interrogates its own claims or the reliability of sources, and alternative readings are not fully considered. The line of argument can be repetitive and occasionally drifts into moral commentary rather than critical evaluation. Consequently, insights, while present, are not consistently sophisticated.

The work meets the descriptors for the 4â6 band but does not demonstrate the sustained critical rigour characteristic of higher bands. A score of 4 reflects competent but limited discussion and evaluation.

**Strengths**: The essay moves beyond narration to compare and contrast the protagonistsâ motivations, childhoods, and moral trajectories, often anchoring points with textual quotation. Some interpretative insightsâe.g., linking scent and identity, or situating Bethâs struggle within 1960s feminismâdemonstrate thoughtful analysis and emerging synthesis.

**Limitations**: Analytical threads are not always fully pursued; comments can be assertive rather than evidentially unpacked, and there is repetition across sections. Counter-arguments or alternative interpretations are limited, so conclusions, while generally supported, are not consistently nuanced. Transitions between examples sometimes lapse into summary.

The depth and effectiveness of analysis are therefore **good but uneven**, aligning with the 4â6 band. A mid-band score of 5 is appropriate.

**Strengths**: The essay follows the prescribed EE structure: title page with candidate details, clear table of contents, introduction, distinct thematic chapters, conclusion, and works-cited list. Pagination and section headings aid navigation, and the organisation is logical and easy to follow.

**Limitations**: Paragraphing is occasionally uneven, and some headings could be more analytical (e.g., repeated ellipses in TOC). Minor inconsistencies appear in section numbering and page references. Nonetheless, all mandatory structural elements are present.

Given that the structure is clearly appropriate but not exemplary in polish, the work sits solidly in the 3â4 band; a score of 3 is justified.

**Strengths**: The student demonstrates solid familiarity with both primary texts, accurately recounting plot points and contextualising them within themes of power, obsession, and social alienation. Secondary materials are referenced to support claims (e.g., feminist readings, psychological studies), indicating engagement with external scholarship.

**Limitations**: The knowledge displayed, while clear, remains largely descriptive; theoretical or historical contexts for SÃ¼skind and Tevis are touched on only superficially. Source use is unevenâsome references (e.g., YouTube commentary) contribute little critical weight, and integration of scholarship is more illustrative than deeply synthesized.

Consequently, the work shows **good but not consistently sophisticated understanding and application of sources**, aligning with the 3â4 band; a score of 4 reflects the stronger end of that range without reaching excellence.

**Strengths**: Literary terminology such as âcharacterization,â âmotif,â âsymbolism,â and ânarrative voiceâ is employed, and psychological terms like âdopamineâ and âsuperiority complexâ indicate interdisciplinary awareness. The student generally uses these terms appropriately, aiding clarity of analysis.

**Limitations**: Application is inconsistent; some terms (e.g., âlimerence,â âpsychopathy,â âanimalisticâ) are used loosely or without precise scholarly definition. At points, sweeping psychological claims are made without sufficient theoretical grounding, and citation style for definitions (e.g., Cambridge Dictionary) is rudimentary. This intermittent imprecision prevents the work from reaching consistent accuracy.

Overall, the terminology is mostly accurate but not sustained at a high scholarly level, justifying a mid-band 3 score within the 3â4 range.

**Strengths**: The research questionââHow do Patrick SÃ¼skindâs Perfumeâ¦ and Walter Tevisâs The Queenâs Gambit depict humanityâs obsession with the pursuit of power?ââis explicitly stated, focused on comparison of two primary texts, and consistently revisited in body sections on childhood, alienation, and morality. Each section links back to aspects of the question (depiction of obsession and power), demonstrating clear alignment between question and discussion.

**Limitations**: The wording of the question is broad (âhumanityâs obsessionâ), and some analytical paragraphs drift into plot summary or sociological commentary, slightly diluting the tightness of focus.

Given the clear statement and generally strong linkage but occasional lapses in precision, the strand fits comfortably in the 5â6 range at a score of 5.

annotations.comment: Including a precise word count at 3 999 meets the formatting requirement and demonstrates careful adherence to the EE limit.
This block merges analysis and context indiscriminately; breaking it into shorter, focused paragraphs will improve readability and logical flow.
The conclusion introduces new setting analysis rather than synthesizing established arguments; confine new insights to the body and reserve the conclusion for summary and evaluation.
The bibliography relies on several nonâpeerâreviewed sources; diversifying with more scholarly articles will strengthen the investigationâs academic foundation.
Citation entries are not fully alphabetized (e.g., online essays appear after published works); reorder sources alphabetically to comply with referencing conventions.
The title is precise and effectively frames the comparative study, establishing clear parameters by naming both texts and the central concept of environmental influence on the pursuit of power.
The introduction devotes excessive space to general definitions of power and obsession before specifying the literary focus; consider condensing background to sharpen the essayâs immediate purpose.
The use of popular media sources like YouTube and blogs in critical analysis undermines scholarly rigor; prioritize peerâreviewed research for stronger academic support.
The student effectively identifies and analyses the simile âlike air heavy with an imminent thunderstorm,â demonstrating strong awareness of how figurative language enhances thematic impact.
The thesis statement ends abruptly midâsentence, disrupting clarity; ensure your roadmap is fully articulated to guide the reader from the outset.
Invoking Grenouilleâs superiority complex without theoretical framework weakens the claim; integrate relevant psychological literature to underpin this analysis.
The dotted leaders in the table of contents are inconsistently applied; standardizing leader dots and tab stops would improve navigational clarity.

The research question, while explicit, is broad in its reference to âhumanityâs obsessionâ; narrowing its focus to specific environmental factors or character studies would tighten alignment with your analysis.
This paragraph largely summarizes plot rather than analyzing thematic nuance; shifting focus from narrative recap to critical interpretation will strengthen analytical depth.
The methodology is only implied by listing source types; explicitly justify your selection criteria and methodological approach to demonstrate rigor and transparency.
Underlining section headers departs from formal academic formatting; adopting consistent heading styles will enhance professional presentation.
The analysis of Bethâs theft is wellâcontextualized but stops short of linking the behavior back to themes of power and obsession; make that connection explicit to reinforce your argument.
The shift from Grenouille to Beth in this section lacks clear structural signposting; explicit indicators of comparative focus will improve coherence.
Section titles in the TOC rely on ellipses rather than clear delineation; using consistent formatting for page references enhances professional presentation.
Freudâs ideas are invoked without contextualization or precise citations; situate psychoanalytic theory within its historical and scholarly framework to enhance credibility.
The comparative discussion shows potential but rarely engages counterarguments or alternative readings; incorporating them will yield a more balanced critical evaluation.
Several character motivations are presented as assertions without sufficient textual or scholarly evidence; deeper unpacking of quotations will substantiate your claims.
Relying on Cambridge Dictionary definitions for key concepts indicates limited engagement with scholarly discourse; integrate more academic source definitions to deepen theoretical grounding.
The argument linking pheromonal theory to social ostracism is speculative and unsupported by academic evidence; incorporate peerâreviewed studies to substantiate this claim.
Engagement with an online UKEssays essay lacks any evaluation of its credibility; assess source reliability explicitly to demonstrate critical source selection.
A single paragraph here traverses multiple themes; dividing it into distinct analytical points will aid the readerâs comprehension of each concept.
The analysis of Grenouilleâs withdrawal quote demonstrates nuanced interpretation of diction and imagery, offering a strong example of textâsupported insight.
Underlining the âConclusionâ header here diverges from earlier formatting choices; ensure consistency across all section headings.
References lack a consistent hangingâindent format, which impedes readability; applying uniform indentation per the chosen style guide will improve presentation.
The final entries show inconsistent font size and indentation, which disrupts the visual cohesion of the works cited; standardize all reference formatting.
The shift between Bethâs and Grenouilleâs narratives lacks transition; adding linking sentences will clarify the comparative structure and logic.
